fact: April 27 is exactly 117 days into the year - that's the same number as the atomic number of tennessine, one of the newest elements on the periodic table!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for baby rabbits to be born - they're called kits and they're born completely bald and blind!
The sun rises around 6 AM and sets around 7:30 PM in most of North America on this date, giving you almost 13.5 hours of daylight to play outside.
April 27 falls in the middle of Taurus season in the zodiac calendar.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the heart of autumn when leaves turn brilliant orange and red before falling.
Dandelions are exploding everywhere around this time in northern countries - each fluffy seed head contains about 2,000 seeds ready to fly away!
This date sits exactly 55 days after the spring equinox, which means spring is officially middle-aged by now.
In many temperate regions, morning dew is typically at its heaviest around this time because warm days and cool nights create perfect conditions for water droplets to form on grass.
Earthworms are most active when the ground temperature hits 55-60°F, which often happens around late April in temperate zones.
In northern regions, this is when maple trees typically release their helicopter seeds - those spinning samaras that twirl down like tiny tornadoes.
April 27 usually marks the time when many fish species start their spawning season in northern lakes and rivers.
